message:
feat(coordinator): add compaction reminder config + trace-login command

- Add CompactionReminderConfig to SPMConfig with enabled/prompt/cooldown/regex_override
- Add CompactingBanner pattern to detect Claude's "Conversation compacted" banner
- Add DetectCompactingBanner() and DetectCompactingBannerWithPattern() functions
- Add --json flag for JSON log output in coordinator
- Add caam trace-login command for tracing auth file changes during login
- Close caam-egeq: config toggles for compaction reminder

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"caam-ef8r","title":"Create BurnRateInfo struct and calculation","description":"# Task: Create BurnRateInfo struct and calculation

## Overview
Create data structures and functions to calculate token consumption rate (burn rate) from historical log data.

## Technical Details

### BurnRateInfo Struct
```go
// internal/usage/burnrate.go

type BurnRateInfo struct {
    TokensPerMinute     float64
    TokensPerHour       float64
    PercentPerHour      float64       // How much of limit consumed per hour
    SamplePeriod        time.Duration // How far back we looked
    SampleSize          int           // Number of data points
    Confidence          float64       // 0-1, based on sample quality
    
    ByModel map[string]float64        // Per-model burn rates
}
```

### Calculation Function
```go
func CalculateBurnRate(entries []*logs.LogEntry, window time.Duration) *BurnRateInfo {
    // 1. Filter entries to window (e.g., last 2 hours)
    // 2. Sum total tokens consumed
    // 3. Calculate elapsed time
    // 4. Derive tokens/minute and tokens/hour
    // 5. Calculate confidence based on:
    //    - Sample size (more entries = higher confidence)
    //    - Variance (steady rate = higher confidence)
    //    - Recency (recent data = higher confidence)
}
```

### Integration with UsageInfo
Add BurnRate field to UsageInfo and populate when log data available.

## Dependencies
- Create internal/logs package structure (caam-xz27)
- Implement Claude JSONL log scanner (caam-ivxt)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-01-10T05:18:17.625416838Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T06:57:36.912694036Z","closed_at":"2026-01-10T06:57:36.912694036Z","close_reason":"Closed","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-ef8r","depends_on_id":"caam-ivxt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T05:20:30.003568306Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-ef8r","depends_on_id":"caam-xz27","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T05:20:29.975452786Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"caam-efrn","title":"EPIC: Predictive Pre-Rotation Alerts","description":"# EPIC: Predictive Pre-Rotation

## Strategic Context

The best way to avoid the pain of mid-session rate limit hits is to PREDICT them and switch BEFORE they happen. This epic combines burn rate data from log parsing with real-time rate limit tracking to forecast when limits will be hit and proactively suggest (or auto-trigger) rotation.

## The Value Proposition

Current flow:
1. Work, work, work
2. BAM! Rate limit hit (unexpected)
3. Scramble to switch accounts
4. Lose flow state

Predictive flow:
1. Work, work, work
2. [caam] "You'll hit limit in ~20 minutes. Switch to bob@gmail.com now?"
3. User: "Yes" → seamless switch
4. Continue working uninterrupted

## Technical Components

### 1. Depletion Prediction Engine
Combine:
- Current usage % (from API)
- Burn rate (from log parsing)
- Historical patterns (from DB)

```go
// internal/prediction/depletion.go

type DepletionPrediction struct {
    Profile         string
    CurrentPercent  float64
    BurnRate        *usage.BurnRateInfo
    PredictedTime   time.Time       // When we'll hit 100%
    Confidence      float64         // 0-1 based on data quality
    Warning         PredictionWarning
}

type PredictionWarning int
const (
    WarningNone       PredictionWarning = iota
    WarningLowData    // Not enough historical data
    WarningHighVariance  // Burn rate is highly variable
    WarningApproaching   // Less than 30 minutes remaining
    WarningImminent     // Less than 10 minutes remaining
)

func PredictDepletion(
    currentUsage *usage.UsageInfo,
    burnRate *usage.BurnRateInfo,
    history []usage.DailyUsage,
) *DepletionPrediction {
    // 1. Calculate base prediction from current + burn rate
    // 2. Adjust confidence based on burn rate variance
    // 3. Consider historical patterns (e.g., user is heavier in mornings)
    // 4. Return prediction with confidence interval
}
```

### 2. Alert System
```go
// internal/prediction/alerts.go

type PredictionAlert struct {
    Type        AlertType
    Profile     string
    Message     string
    SuggestedAction string
    TimeUntil   time.Duration
    Urgency     AlertUrgency
}

type AlertType int
const (
    AlertApproachingLimit AlertType = iota
    AlertImminentLimit
    AlertSwitchRecommended
    AlertAllProfilesLow
)

type AlertUrgency int
const (
    UrgencyInfo AlertUrgency = iota
    UrgencyWarning
    UrgencyCritical
)
```

### 3. Integration with caam run
```go
// In caam run wrapper

func (r *SmartRunner) monitorPredictions(ctx context.Context) {
    ticker := time.NewTicker(1 * time.Minute)
    defer ticker.Stop()
    
    for {
        select {
        case <-ctx.Done():
            return
        case <-ticker.C:
            pred := r.predictor.Predict(r.currentProfile)
            if pred.Warning >= WarningApproaching {
                r.showPredictionAlert(pred)
            }
        }
    }
}
```

### 4. Pre-Rotation Suggestions
```go
type PreRotationSuggestion struct {
    FromProfile    string
    ToProfile      string
    Reason         string
    OptimalSwitchTime time.Time
    Alternatives   []string
}

func SuggestRotation(
    profiles []*PooledProfile,
    predictions map[string]*DepletionPrediction,
) *PreRotationSuggestion {
    // Find profile that will deplete soonest
    // Find best backup profile
    // Calculate optimal switch time (before current hits limit)
    // Return suggestion
}
```

## User Experience

### In caam run
```
$ caam run claude -- "implement feature X"

[session running...]

[caam] ⚠️  PREDICTION: You'll hit rate limit in ~23 minutes
       Current profile: alice@gmail.com (78% used)
       Burn rate: 2.5%/minute (based on last hour)
       
       Recommendation: Switch to bob@gmail.com (12% used)
       
       [S]witch now | [L]ater | [I]gnore this session
```

### In caam limits --forecast
```
$ caam limits --forecast

╔═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║                    USAGE FORECAST                                  ║
╠═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
║ claude/alice@gmail.com                                             ║
║   Current: 78%  │  Burn rate: 2.5%/min  │  Depletes in: ~23 min   ║
║   ⚠️  Switch recommended                                           ║
║                                                                     ║
║ claude/bob@gmail.com                                                ║
║   Current: 12%  │  Burn rate: N/A (idle)  │  Headroom: 4+ hours   ║
║   ✅ Ready as backup                                                ║
║                                                                     ║
║ RECOMMENDATION: Switch to bob now for uninterrupted work           ║
╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
```

## Deliverables
1. Depletion prediction engine
2. Alert system with urgency levels
3. Integration with caam run (background monitoring)
4. Pre-rotation suggestion algorithm
5. Enhanced --forecast flag in limits command
6. User notification/prompt system

## Dependencies
- EPIC: Log File Parsing (for burn rate calculation)
- EPIC: Enhanced Rate Limit Tracking (for tertiary windows, burn rate)

## Enables
- Proactive (vs reactive) account rotation
- User never unexpectedly hits rate limits
- Optimal utilization of all accounts","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-10T05:14:29.258892060Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T01:24:47.199225660Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T01:24:47.199225660Z","close_reason":"Implemented: Depletion prediction engine (internal/prediction/engine.go), Alert system with urgency levels (alerts.go), Pre-rotation suggestion algorithm, and --forecast flag in limits command. All tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-efrn","depends_on_id":"caam-3qdv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T05:20:48.592501927Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-efrn","depends_on_id":"caam-4zvq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T05:20:48.561777941Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"caam-ela","title":"Implement device code auth for Codex provider","description":"# Pass --device-auth to Codex in Profile Isolation Mode

## Scope

When \`caam login codex <profile> --device-code\` is called, pass \`--device-auth\` to the underlying \`codex login\` command.

## Note: Primary Workflow Doesn't Need This

For the main auth file swapping workflow:
\`\`\`bash
codex login --device-auth    # Native command - already works!
caam backup codex account1   # Existing caam command
\`\`\`

This task is only for Profile Isolation mode (\`caam login\` / \`caam exec\`).

## Implementation

In internal/provider/codex/codex.go, update Login() to accept options:

\`\`\`go
type LoginOptions struct {
    DeviceCode bool
}

func (p *Provider) Login(ctx context.Context, prof *profile.Profile, opts LoginOptions) error {
    if opts.DeviceCode {
        return p.loginWithDeviceCode(ctx, prof)
    }
    // ... existing logic
}

func (p *Provider) loginWithDeviceCode(ctx context.Context, prof *profile.Profile) error {
    cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, "codex", "login", "--device-auth")
    cmd.Env = append(os.Environ(), "CODEX_HOME="+prof.CodexHomePath())
    cmd.Stdout = os.Stdout
    cmd.Stderr = os.Stderr
    cmd.Stdin = os.Stdin
    return cmd.Run()
}
\`\`\`

## Priority: P2

Dependency of caam-v89. Only useful for Profile Isolation mode.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-17T22:36:47.927885810Z","updated_at":"2025-12-18T01:51:47.783208279Z","closed_at":"2025-12-18T01:51:47.783208279Z","close_reason":"Codex provider now supports device code login (codex login --device-auth) for isolated profiles via provider.DeviceCodeProvider.","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-ela","depends_on_id":"caam-hzu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T22:40:02.781756600Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":29,"issue_id":"caam-ela","author":"ubuntu","text":"Working in tandem with caam-v89: implement provider.DeviceCodeProvider for Codex and wire `caam login codex <profile> --device-code` to run `codex login --device-auth` inside the profile CODEX_HOME.","created_at":"2025-12-18T01:48:34Z"},{"id":31,"issue_id":"caam-ela","author":"ubuntu","text":"Landed in ff48b6d: Codex provider implements DeviceCodeProvider and runs `codex login --device-auth` with CODEX_HOME set to the isolated profile.","created_at":"2025-12-18T01:51:57Z"}]}
 {"id":"caam-esb1","title":"E2E: Bundle export-encrypt-import workflow","description":"# E2E Test: Bundle Export-Encrypt-Import Workflow

## Goal
Test complete bundle lifecycle with encryption.

## Workflow Steps

### 1. Setup
- Create vault with multiple profiles
- Configure bundle settings

### 2. Export Phase
```
bundle export --output backup.bundle
verify bundle created
verify manifest.json correct
verify all profiles included
```

### 3. Encrypted Export
```
bundle export --output encrypted.bundle --encrypt --password "test123"
verify encryption metadata present
verify bundle is encrypted
```

### 4. Import to Fresh Vault
```
clear vault (new temp directory)
bundle import backup.bundle
verify all profiles restored
verify auth files match original
```

### 5. Encrypted Import
```
clear vault
bundle import encrypted.bundle --password "test123"
verify decryption successful
verify profiles restored
```

### 6. Error Scenarios
```
bundle import encrypted.bundle --password "wrong"
verify error: decryption failed

bundle import corrupted.bundle
verify error: validation failed
```

## Test File
internal/e2e/workflows/bundle_workflow_test.go

## Logging Requirements
- Log bundle contents (file list)
- Log encryption parameters (not keys)
- Log import progress
- Log validation results

## Acceptance Criteria
- Export creates valid bundle
- Encryption works correctly
- Import restores all data
- Errors handled gracefully
- Detailed logs for debugging","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-20T00:06:59.247456459Z","updated_at":"2025-12-20T01:43:14.792337012Z","closed_at":"2025-12-20T01:43:14.792337012Z","close_reason":"E2E bundle export-encrypt-import workflow tests implemented and passing. Tests cover: full lifecycle (export, encrypt, import, decrypt), round-trip data integrity verification, dry-run functionality, and error scenarios (wrong password, missing password, nonexistent bundle).","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-esb1","depends_on_id":"caam-ldjp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-20T00:12:48.742704599Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"caam-tg77.5","title":"Task: Gum-enhanced CLI flows + fallback UX","description":"Goal
- Make non-TUI CLI interactions feel premium while remaining scriptable.

Details
- Use gum for optional interactive prompts (confirmations, choices, input) in commands like setup, pick, backup, and update.
- Detect TTY and NO_COLOR; fall back to plain text prompts automatically.
- Provide a `--no-gum` / `--plain` mode for automation.
- Ensure outputs are stable for scripts when `--json` or `--plain` is set.
- Emit structured logs for each prompt step (action, choice, result) in debug mode.

Tests
- Simulate gum present/absent and CI environments to verify fallback behavior.
- Snapshot tests for prompt text in plain mode.
- E2E test for scripted automation path (no prompts).

Success
- CLI flows feel polished without breaking automation.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:56:43.651804927Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T21:54:13.670085952Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-tg77.5","depends_on_id":"caam-tg77","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:56:43.653147215Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"caam-tg77.6","title":"Task: Claude config-dir isolation mode (CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR) + fallback","description":"Goal
- Support a robust, industrial-grade CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR isolation mode with safe fallbacks.

Background
- A community gist suggests per-account config directories via CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR and symlinked shared items.
- Official docs emphasize ~/.claude and .claude project dirs; CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR is not clearly documented and has open bugs.

Details
- Implement a Go-based "config-dir manager" that:
  - lays out per-profile config dirs consistently
  - validates dir health (permissions, required files, no unexpected cross-links)
  - selects isolation strategy: env (CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR) vs HOME/XDG
- Add config flag: claude.config_dir_mode = auto|env|home (default auto).
- In auto mode: set CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR if supported/healthy; otherwise use HOME + XDG_CONFIG_HOME isolation.
- Add a compatibility probe (version + behavior check) and warn if CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR still creates local .claude dirs or breaks /ide.
- Ensure isolation logic is consistent across caam exec, setup, and run wrappers.
- Emit structured logs that indicate which isolation strategy was selected and why.

Tests
- Unit tests for env selection logic and config parsing.
- File-system tests for config-dir manager health checks.
- E2E test using a mocked claude binary that prints env vars to ensure correct propagation.
- Regression test for fallback when CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR is flagged unhealthy.

Success
- Users can adopt the CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR strategy when it works, without breaking workflows when it does not.
","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T01:34:28.111743337Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-21T21:35:04.920962689Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-tg77.6","depends_on_id":"caam-tg77","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-17T01:34:28.113103509Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"caam-tg77.7","title":"Task: Claude shared-items policy (allowlist + symlinks)","description":"Goal
- Let users explicitly share safe Claude assets (agents, commands, styles) across isolated profiles without sharing history/projects.

Background
- Community gist suggests symlinking selected directories via a shared_items allowlist.
- Sharing history/projects between accounts can cause confusing cross-account state.

Details
- Implement a Go "shared-items manager" with:
  - allowlist + hard denylist (projects/history/sessions/logs never shared)
  - atomic symlink creation and rollback on failure
  - conflict handling: if a real dir exists, back it up before linking
  - optional copy fallback when symlinks are not permitted
- Add config: claude.shared_items = ["agents", "commands", "styles", "plugins", "skills"] (default empty).
- Provide CLI helpers: `caam claude share --list/--add/--remove` with dry‑run.
- Emit structured logs for each symlink created, skipped, or rejected by policy.

Tests
- Unit tests for allowlist/denylist validation.
- Filesystem tests that verify symlink creation, backup, rollback, and copy fallback.
- E2E test: repeated runs are idempotent and preserve existing files.

Success
- Users can safely share reusable assets without leaking account history or state.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T01:34:38.953587681Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T01:37:42.072693931Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-tg77.7","depends_on_id":"caam-tg77","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-17T01:34:38.955143250Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-tg77.7","depends_on_id":"caam-tg77.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T01:35:00.651342487Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"caam-tg77.8","title":"Task: Claude login file-diff tracer (discover all touched paths)","description":"Goal
- Provide a safe tool to discover exactly which Claude files change during login/logout.

Details
- Add `caam claude trace-login` (or `caam trace claude`) that:
  - snapshots candidate dirs (~/.claude, ~/.config/claude-code, project .claude)
  - pauses for user to perform login/logout
  - produces a diff report (added/changed/removed files + sizes + hashes)
- Support `--exclude` patterns and a `--no-projects` flag to avoid large repo scans.
- Output a JSON report for tooling + a human summary table.
- Emit a derived "watch rules" section listing the minimal file set for caam watch.
- Never deletes files; uses read-only snapshots.

Tests
- Unit tests for diff computation on synthetic directories.
- E2E test using a temp dir to simulate changes and verifying report output + watch rules.
- Log output tests to ensure sensitive content is never printed.

Success
- Users can empirically confirm which files need isolation or backup.

 {"id":"caam-thb","title":"Token expiry parsing for all providers","description":"# Token Expiry Parsing

## Purpose
Parse token expiration timestamps from each provider authentication files to determine how long tokens remain valid.

## Background
Each provider stores auth differently. We need to extract expiry info from:
1. Claude Code - oauth.json with expiry field
2. OpenAI Codex - auth.json with different structure
3. Google Gemini - gcloud ADC format

## Technical Requirements

### Parser Interface
```go
type ExpiryParser interface {
    ParseExpiry(authFilePath string) (time.Time, error)
}

func ParseClaudeExpiry(path string) (time.Time, error)
func ParseCodexExpiry(path string) (time.Time, error)
func ParseGeminiExpiry(path string) (time.Time, error)
```

### Provider-Specific Formats

#### Claude Code
```json
// ~/.claude.ai/oauth.json (example, needs verification)
{
  "access_token": "...",
  "refresh_token": "...",
  "expires_at": "2025-12-17T18:00:00Z",
  "token_type": "Bearer"
}
```
Alternative: `expires_in` (seconds) + issued time

#### OpenAI Codex
```json
// ~/.codex/auth.json (example, needs verification)
{
  "access_token": "...",
  "refresh_token": "...",
  "expires_at": 1734451200,  // Unix timestamp
  "token_type": "Bearer"
}
```

#### Google Gemini (ADC)
```json
// ~/.config/gcloud/application_default_credentials.json
{
  "client_id": "...",
  "client_secret": "...",
  "refresh_token": "...",
  "type": "authorized_user"
}
```
Note: ADC may not store expiry - needs refresh to get expiry.

### Fallback Strategy
If expiry cannot be determined:
1. Check if refresh_token exists → assume valid but unknown expiry
2. No refresh_token → mark as "needs login"
3. Return sentinel value for "unknown expiry"

## Implementation Notes
- Study actual auth file formats (may need reverse engineering)
- Handle missing files gracefully
- Handle malformed JSON
- Support both Unix timestamps and ISO8601

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] ParseClaudeExpiry implemented and tested
- [ ] ParseCodexExpiry implemented and tested  
- [ ] ParseGeminiExpiry implemented and tested
- [ ] Handles missing files (returns error)
- [ ] Handles malformed JSON (returns error)
- [ ] Unit tests with fixture files

## Files to Create/Modify
- `internal/health/expiry.go` (new)
- `internal/health/expiry_test.go` (new)
- `internal/health/testdata/` (fixtures)

## Research Needed
- [ ] Verify Claude Code auth file format
- [ ] Verify Codex auth file format
- [ ] Determine if Gemini ADC includes expiry

## Dependencies
None","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:17:51.236078340Z","updated_at":"2025-12-17T21:44:20.264399828Z","closed_at":"2025-12-17T21:44:20.264399828Z","close_reason":"Implemented token expiry parsing for Claude, Codex, and Gemini providers.  {"id":"caam-wuc","title":"Project Context","description":"# Project Context (Directory-Profile Association)

## Purpose
Remember which profile a user prefers for each project directory, enabling automatic or suggested profile activation based on working directory.

## Background (from codex-pool)
codex-pool pins conversations to accounts:
```go
if conversationID != "" {
    if id, ok := p.convPin[conversationID]; ok {
        return p.getLocked(id)
    }
}
```

## Adaptation for caam
Reframed as "project-profile association":
- Associate directories with profiles
- Suggest or auto-activate based on CWD
- Support multi-provider associations

## Use Cases
1. **Multi-client freelancer**: Different clients = different accounts
2. **Work/personal separation**: Work projects use work account
3. **Team projects**: Shared projects use team account
4. **Open source vs enterprise**: Different profiles for different contexts

## What This Epic Delivers
1. Project-profile association storage
2. Working directory detection
3. Association CLI commands
4. Integration with `caam activate`
5. TUI project indicator (optional)

## Storage Format
```json
// ~/.caam/projects.json
{
  "associations": {
    "/home/user/work/client-a": {
      "claude": "client-a@work.com",
      "codex": "work-main"
    },
    "/home/user/personal/blog": {
      "claude": "personal@gmail.com"
    }
  },
  "settings": {
    "auto_activate": false,
    "suggest_on_mismatch": true
  }
}
```

## Matching Strategy
1. Exact match: `/home/user/work/client-a`
2. Parent directory: If CWD is `/home/user/work/client-a/src`, match parent
3. Glob patterns (future): `/home/user/work/*` → work profile

## User Experience
```bash
# Associate current directory with a profile
$ cd /home/user/work/client-a
$ caam project set claude client-a@work.com
Associated with claude/client-a@work.com

# List associations
$ caam project list
/home/user/work/client-a
  claude: client-a@work.com
  codex:  work-main

# Activate with suggestion
$ cd /home/user/work/client-a
$ caam activate claude
Using project association: client-a@work.com
Activated.

# Mismatch warning
$ caam activate claude personal@gmail.com
Warning: Project associated with client-a@work.com
Continue with personal@gmail.com? [y/N]
```

## Configuration Options
- `auto_activate`: Automatically use association (no prompt)
- `suggest_on_mismatch`: Warn when activating non-associated profile
- `inherit_parent`: Check parent directories for associations

## Dependencies
- Profile Health Foundation (for status display)

## Future Enhancements
- Git remote-based associations
- Team-shared associations (via git)
- IDE integrations","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:16:44.984411767Z","updated_at":"2025-12-18T01:46:42.732418376Z","closed_at":"2025-12-18T01:46:42.732418376Z","close_reason":"Fully implemented: project association storage (store.go with Resolve, glob patterns, parent directory inheritance), CLI commands (project set/list/show/remove/clear), activate integration (resolveActivateProfile). All tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-wuc","depends_on_id":"caam-3am","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:26:58.772622139Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"caam-wxaz","title":"Implement JWT parser for Codex auth tokens","description":"# Task: Implement JWT parser for Codex auth tokens

## Overview
Parse JWT ID tokens from Codex auth.json to extract identity information (email, plan, org) without making API calls.

## Background
Codex auth.json contains an `id_token` which is a JWT (JSON Web Token). JWTs have three base64-encoded parts: header.payload.signature. The payload contains claims like email, plan_type, organization.

We do NOT need to verify the signature - we trust the token since it came from the provider.

## Technical Details

### JWT Payload Extraction
```go
// internal/identity/jwt.go

func ParseJWT(token string) (map[string]any, error) {
    parts := strings.Split(token, ".")
    if len(parts) != 3 {
        return nil, errors.New("invalid JWT format")
    }
    
    // Decode payload (second part)
    payload, err := base64.RawURLEncoding.DecodeString(parts[1])
    if err != nil {
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("decode payload: %w", err)
    }
    
    var claims map[string]any
    if err := json.Unmarshal(payload, &claims); err != nil {
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("parse claims: %w", err)
    }
    
    return claims, nil
}
```

### Identity Extraction
```go
type Identity struct {
    Email        string    `json:"email,omitempty"`
    Organization string    `json:"organization,omitempty"`
    PlanType     string    `json:"plan_type,omitempty"`
    AccountID    string    `json:"account_id,omitempty"`
    ExpiresAt    time.Time `json:"expires_at,omitempty"`
    Provider     string    `json:"provider"`
}

func ExtractFromCodexAuth(authPath string) (*Identity, error) {
    // 1. Read auth.json
    // 2. Extract id_token or tokens.id_token
    // 3. Parse JWT claims
    // 4. Map claims to Identity struct
}
```

## Files to Create
- internal/identity/identity.go
- internal/identity/jwt.go
- internal/identity/codex.go
- internal/identity/jwt_test.go","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-10T05:17:53.178394075Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T05:54:20.131746143Z","closed_at":"2026-01-10T05:54:20.131746143Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of caam-vek1","compaction_level":0}
 {"id":"caam-x0g","title":"Profile Health Foundation","description":"# Profile Health Foundation

## Purpose
Establish the infrastructure for tracking and displaying profile health status. This is the foundation that token refresh and health display features build upon.

## Background (from codex-pool)
codex-pool uses a sophisticated scoring algorithm:
```go
score = headroom * planBonus * creditBonus - penalty
```

For caam v1, we simplify to status tiers (🟢🟡🔴) with numeric scores as internal implementation.

## What This Epic Delivers
1. Health metadata storage format and persistence
2. Token expiry parsing for all three providers
3. Health status calculation logic
4. Penalty tracking with decay

## Design Decisions

### Status Tiers (User-Facing)
| Status | Icon | Meaning |
|--------|------|---------|
| Healthy | 🟢 | Token valid >1hr, no recent errors |
| Warning | 🟡 | Token expiring <1hr OR recent errors |
| Critical | 🔴 | Token expired OR many recent errors |
| Unknown | ⚪ | Cannot determine status |

### Health Factors (Internal Scoring)
1. Token expiry: +1.0 (>1hr), +0.5 (15-60min), 0 (<15min), -1.0 (expired)
2. Recent errors: +0.3 (none), 0 (1-2), -0.5 (3+)
3. Plan type bonus: +0.2 (pro), +0.3 (enterprise)

### Penalty Decay (from codex-pool)
```go
// Every 5 minutes:
penalty *= 0.8
if penalty < 0.01 { penalty = 0 }
```
This prevents permanent blacklisting while discouraging repeated use of problematic profiles.

## Storage Format
```json
// ~/.caam/health.json
{
  "profiles": {
    "claude/work@company.com": {
      "token_expires_at": "2025-12-17T18:00:00Z",
      "last_error": "2025-12-17T15:30:00Z",
      "error_count_1h": 2,
      "penalty": 0.3,
      "penalty_updated_at": "2025-12-17T15:35:00Z",
      "plan_type": "pro",
      "last_checked": "2025-12-17T16:00:00Z"
    }
  },
  "version": 1
}
```

## Dependencies
- None (foundation layer)

## Blocks
- Token Refresh (needs expiry info)
- Health Display (needs status calculation)
- Penalty Decay (needs penalty storage)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:16:17.755807834Z","updated_at":"2025-12-18T01:43:02.819075085Z","closed_at":"2025-12-18T01:43:02.819075085Z","close_reason":"Fully implemented by previous agents: health storage (storage.go), token expiry parsing for all providers (expiry.go), health status calculation (status.go), penalty decay (penalty.go), CLI integration (root.go status/ls commands), formatting (format.go). All tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-x0g","depends_on_id":"caam-3am","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:26:38.348841528Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

+			output:       "CONVERSATION COMPACTED · CTRL+O for history",
+			wantDetected: true,
+			wantContains: "CONVERSATION COMPACTED",
+			desc:         "All uppercase should match (case insensitive)",
+		},
+		{
+			name:         "case insensitive - mixed case",
+			output:       "ConVersation ComPacted · Ctrl+O for history",
+			wantDetected: true,
+			wantContains: "ConVersation ComPacted",
+			desc:         "Mixed case should match",
+		},
+		{
+			name:         "no match - unrelated text",
+			output:       "Normal terminal output here",
+			wantDetected: false,
+			wantContains: "",
+			desc:         "Unrelated text should not match",
+		},
+		{
+			name:         "no match - partial text",
+			output:       "Conversation compacted",
+			wantDetected: false,
+			wantContains: "",
+			desc:         "Partial match without ctrl+o should not match",
+		},
+		{
+			name:         "no match - ctrl+o without compacted",
+			output:       "Press ctrl+o for history",
+			wantDetected: false,
+			wantContains: "",
+			desc:         "ctrl+o alone should not match",
+		},
+		{
+			name:         "banner in multiline output",
+			output:       "Some previous output\nConversation compacted · ctrl+o for history\nSome following output",
+			wantDetected: true,
+			wantContains: "Conversation compacted",
+			desc:         "Banner embedded in multiline output",
+		},
+		{
+			name:         "banner at end of output",
+			output:       "Long conversation history here...\n\nConversation compacted · ctrl+o for history",
+			wantDetected: true,
+			wantContains: "Conversation compacted",
+			desc:         "Banner at end of output",
+		},
+	}
+
+	for _, tt := range tests {
+		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
+			detected, matchedText := DetectCompactingBanner(tt.output)
+			if detected != tt.wantDetected {
+				t.Errorf("DetectCompactingBanner() detected = %v, want %v\nDescription: %s", detected, tt.wantDetected, tt.desc)
+			}
+			if tt.wantDetected && tt.wantContains != "" {
+				if matchedText == "" || !contains(matchedText, tt.wantContains) {
+					t.Errorf("DetectCompactingBanner() matchedText = %q, want to contain %q", matchedText, tt.wantContains)
+				}
+			}
+			t.Logf("fixture=%s detected=%v matched_length=%d", tt.name, detected, len(matchedText))
+		})
+	}
+}

+func TestDetectCompactingBanner_WithANSI(t *testing.T) {
+	tests := []struct {
+		name         string
+		output       string
+		wantDetected bool
+		desc         string
+	}{
+		{
+			name:         "banner with color codes",
+			output:       "\x1b[36mConversation compacted\x1b[0m · ctrl+o for history",
+			wantDetected: true,
+			desc:         "Banner with cyan color codes",
+		},
+		{
+			name:         "banner with bold",
+			output:       "\x1b[1mConversation compacted · ctrl+o for history\x1b[0m",
+			wantDetected: true,
+			desc:         "Banner with bold formatting",
+		},
+		{
+			name:         "banner with 256-color",
+			output:       "\x1b[38;5;208mConversation compacted · ctrl+o for history\x1b[0m",
+			wantDetected: true,
+			desc:         "Banner with 256-color foreground",
+		},
+		{
+			name:         "banner with RGB color",
+			output:       "\x1b[38;2;100;150;200mConversation compacted · ctrl+o for history\x1b[0m",
+			wantDetected: true,
+			desc:         "Banner with true-color (RGB) formatting",
+		},
+		{
+			name:         "banner with cursor movement",
+			output:       "\x1b[2K\x1b[1GConversation compacted · ctrl+o for history",
+			wantDetected: true,
+			desc:         "Banner after cursor clear and move",
+		},
+		{
+			name:         "banner with stacked ANSI codes",
+			output:       "\x1b[1m\x1b[4m\x1b[36mConversation compacted\x1b[0m\x1b[0m · ctrl+o for history\x1b[0m",
+			wantDetected: true,
+			desc:         "Banner with multiple stacked ANSI codes",
+		},
+		{
+			name: "banner in styled terminal box",
+			output: "\x1b[36m╭───────────────────────────────────────╮\x1b[0m\n" +
+				"\x1b[36m│\x1b[0m Conversation compacted · ctrl+o for history \x1b[36m│\x1b[0m\n" +
+				"\x1b[36m╰───────────────────────────────────────╯\x1b[0m",
+			wantDetected: true,
+			desc:         "Banner in styled terminal box with box-drawing characters",
+		},
+		{
+			name:         "banner with ANSI in middle of text",
+			output:       "Conversation \x1b[1mcompacted\x1b[0m · ctrl+o for history",
+			wantDetected: true,
+			desc:         "ANSI codes within banner text",
+		},
+		{
+			name:         "complex terminal output with banner",
+			output:       "\x1b[2J\x1b[H\x1b[?25l\x1b[1;1HConversation compacted · ctrl+o for history\x1b[?25h",
+			wantDetected: true,
+			desc:         "Banner with screen clear and cursor visibility codes",
+		},
+	}
+
+	for _, tt := range tests {
+		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
+			detected, matchedText := DetectCompactingBanner(tt.output)
+			if detected != tt.wantDetected {
+				t.Errorf("DetectCompactingBanner() detected = %v, want %v\nDescription: %s", detected, tt.wantDetected, tt.desc)
+			}
+			t.Logf("fixture=%s detected=%v matched=%q", tt.name, detected, matchedText)
+		})
+	}
+}

+func TestDetectCompactingBanner_EdgeCases(t *testing.T) {
+	tests := []struct {
+		name         string
+		output       string
+		wantDetected bool
+		desc         string
+	}{
+		{
+			name:         "empty string",
+			output:       "",
+			wantDetected: false,
+			desc:         "Empty string should not match",
+		},
+		{
+			name:         "only whitespace",
+			output:       "   \n\t\n   ",
+			wantDetected: false,
+			desc:         "Whitespace only should not match",
+		},
+		{
+			name:         "banner-like but wrong order",
+			output:       "ctrl+o for history · Conversation compacted",
+			wantDetected: false,
+			desc:         "Reversed order should not match",
+		},
+		{
+			name:         "typo in 'compacted'",
+			output:       "Conversation compactd · ctrl+o for history",
+			wantDetected: false,
+			desc:         "Typo in 'compacted' should not match",
+		},
+		{
+			name:         "very long output with banner",
+			output:       string(make([]byte, 10000)) + "Conversation compacted · ctrl+o for history" + string(make([]byte, 10000)),
+			wantDetected: true,
+			desc:         "Banner in very long output should still be found",
+		},
+		{
+			name:         "multiple banners",
+			output:       "Conversation compacted · ctrl+o for history\nConversation compacted · ctrl+o for history",
+			wantDetected: true,
+			desc:         "Multiple banners - first one should match",
+		},
+		{
+			name:         "unicode whitespace around banner",
+			output:       "\u00A0Conversation compacted · ctrl+o for history\u00A0",
+			wantDetected: true,
+			desc:         "Non-breaking spaces around banner",
+		},
+	}
+
+	for _, tt := range tests {
+		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
+			detected, _ := DetectCompactingBanner(tt.output)
+			if detected != tt.wantDetected {
+				t.Errorf("DetectCompactingBanner() detected = %v, want %v\nDescription: %s", detected, tt.wantDetected, tt.desc)
+			}
+		})
+	}
+}
+
+func TestDetectCompactingBannerWithPattern(t *testing.T) {
+	customPattern := regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)custom\s+compacting\s+message`)
+
+	tests := []struct {
+		name         string
+		output       string
+		pattern      *regexp.Regexp
+		wantDetected bool
+		desc         string
+	}{
+		{
+			name:         "nil pattern uses default",
+			output:       "Conversation compacted · ctrl+o for history",
+			pattern:      nil,
+			wantDetected: true,
+			desc:         "nil pattern should fall back to default",
+		},
+		{
+			name:         "custom pattern matches",
+			output:       "Custom compacting message here",
+			pattern:      customPattern,
+			wantDetected: true,
+			desc:         "Custom pattern should match custom text",
+		},
+		{
+			name:         "custom pattern does not match default text",
+			output:       "Conversation compacted · ctrl+o for history",
+			pattern:      customPattern,
+			wantDetected: false,
+			desc:         "Custom pattern should not match default banner",
+		},
+		{
+			name:         "default pattern does not match custom text",
+			output:       "Custom compacting message here",
+			pattern:      nil,
+			wantDetected: false,
+			desc:         "Default pattern should not match custom text",
+		},
+	}
+
+	for _, tt := range tests {
+		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
+			detected, _ := DetectCompactingBannerWithPattern(tt.output, tt.pattern)
+			if detected != tt.wantDetected {
+				t.Errorf("DetectCompactingBannerWithPattern() detected = %v, want %v\nDescription: %s", detected, tt.wantDetected, tt.desc)
+			}
+		})
+	}
+}
+
+func TestCompactingBannerPattern_Regex(t *testing.T) {
+	tests := []struct {
+		name      string
+		input     string
+		wantMatch bool
+		desc      string
+	}{
+		{
+			name:      "exact standard format",
+			input:     "Conversation compacted · ctrl+o for history",
+			wantMatch: true,
+			desc:      "Standard format matches",
+		},
+		{
+			name:      "without 'for history' suffix",
+			input:     "Conversation compacted · ctrl+o",
+			wantMatch: true,
+			desc:      "Pattern only requires up to ctrl+o",
+		},
+		{
+			name:      "conversation alone",
+			input:     "Conversation",
+			wantMatch: false,
+			desc:      "Just 'Conversation' should not match",
+		},
+		{
+			name:      "compacted alone",
+			input:     "compacted",
+			wantMatch: false,
+			desc:      "Just 'compacted' should not match",
+		},
+		{
+			name:      "different casing",
+			input:     "conversation COMPACTED · CTRL+O",
+			wantMatch: true,
+			desc:      "Mixed case should match due to (?i) flag",
+		},
+	}
+
+	for _, tt := range tests {
+		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
+			// Test on ANSI-stripped output (as the detection functions do)
+			normalizedInput := StripANSI(tt.input)
+			match := Patterns.CompactingBanner.MatchString(normalizedInput)
+			if match != tt.wantMatch {
+				t.Errorf("CompactingBanner.MatchString(%q) = %v, want %v\nDescription: %s", tt.input, match, tt.wantMatch, tt.desc)
+			}
+		})
+	}
+}
+
+// contains is a helper function for checking substring presence
+func contains(s, substr string) bool {
+	return len(s) >= len(substr) && (s == substr || len(substr) == 0 ||
+		(len(s) > 0 && len(substr) > 0 && findSubstring(s, substr)))
+}
+
+func findSubstring(s, substr string) bool {
+	for i := 0; i <= len(s)-len(substr); i++ {
+		if s[i:i+len(substr)] == substr {
+			return true
+		}
+	}
+	return false
+}
